prompt: Acting as a senior hardware product manager, conduct in-depth research on tablet-style devices used for payments or SaaS applications. Your report should: 1) List major manufacturers, specific device models, and their configurations. 2) Include images of these devices. 3) Analyze the primary use cases and scenarios where these devices are deployed. 4) Investigate the market penetration, common usage scenarios, typical price ranges, and estimated installed base for such devices across different regions (North America, Japan/Korea, Southeast Asia, South America).
article:
# Tablet-Style Devices for Payments and SaaS Applications: Manufacturers, Models, Use Cases, and Regional Market Assessment

## Executive Summary

This report defines **tablet-style devices for payments or SaaS applications** as touchscreen-first business endpoints that either (a) directly accept payments or (b) run in-person business software such as POS, ordering, field service, healthcare, logistics, or appointment workflows. In practice, the market breaks into four commercially distinct classes: **dedicated payment tablets / SmartPOS**, **tablet POS terminals**, **rugged enterprise tablets used with SaaS**, and **consumer tablets used as business endpoints with external payment peripherals** [[1]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/main-navigation/solutions/merchants/solutions-and-services/terminals/android-smartpos)[[2]](https://ingenico.com/us-en/products-services/payment-terminals/axium-android/axium-ex6000)[[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/)[[4]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware)[[5]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/pos-terminal-countertop-kit-for-usb-c-tablets)[[6]](https://www.elotouch.com/pos-terminals.html)[[7]](https://www.honeywell.com/us/en/news/featured-stories/2021/02/how-field-operations-use-enterprise-rugged-tablets-to-gain-productivity)[[8]](https://rmsomega.com/technology/mobile-computing/tablets/)[[9]](https://www.zebra.com/content/dam/zebra_dam/en/spec-sheets/et60-et65-spec-sheet-en-us.pdf)[[10]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/choosing-your-pos-devices-for-multi-location-retail)[[11]](https://www.shopify.com/pos/ipad-pos)[[12]](https://www.samsung.com/us/business/mobile/tablets/galaxy-tab-active/explore/)[[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html).

The most important strategic split is not simply by hardware type, but by **control point in the commercial stack**. **Payment-first platforms**—notably **Clover/Fiserv, PAX, Ingenico, Verifone, and acquirer-led bundles such as PayCAS Mobile**—win where merchants or channels prioritize certified payment acceptance, rapid deployment, remote device control, and processor integration [[1]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/main-navigation/solutions/merchants/solutions-and-services/terminals/android-smartpos)[[2]](https://ingenico.com/us-en/products-services/payment-terminals/axium-android/axium-ex6000)[[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/)[[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[33]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices)[[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[40]](https://connect.verifone.com/en/us/verifone-victa/verifone-victa-mobile)[[41]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2023-03/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM%20DX8000_ING_230210.pdf)[[47]](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/01/09/3007276/0/en/verifone-unveils-new-payment-device-portfolio-and-innovations-to-revolutionize-commerce.html)[[85]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/a920pro)[[88]](https://www.pax.us/products/)[[209]](https://www.paycas.jp/paycas-mobile). **SaaS- or workflow-first platforms**—notably **Square, Toast, Shopify, Elo, Zebra, Honeywell, Samsung, and SUNMI**—win where the device is expected to run a broader operational workflow: ordering, inventory, line-busting, kitchen routing, self-service, proof-of-delivery, or field operations [[4]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware)[[5]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/pos-terminal-countertop-kit-for-usb-c-tablets)[[10]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/choosing-your-pos-devices-for-multi-location-retail)[[14]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/shopify-pos-terminal)[[15]](https://www.elotouch.com/pos-terminals-elopos-z20.html)[[16]](https://hardware.shopify.com/pages/poshub)[[17]](https://support.toasttab.com/en/article/Toast-Hardware-Hub)[[19]](https://pos.toasttab.com/ie/products/handheld-pos)[[21]](https://d2c9w5yn32a2ju.cloudfront.net/knowledgebase/Toast-Hardware_Specs_DIGITAL.pdf)[[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)[[46]](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nyse-tost/toast/annual-reports/nyse-tost-2025-10K-25670997.pdf)[[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html)[[79]](https://pos.toasttab.com/hardware)[[87]](https://www.sunmi.com/en/d3-pro/)[[144]](https://www.elotouch.com/mobile-computers-elo-pay-m100-mobile-pos-tablet.html)[[147]](https://www.samsung.com/ca/business/tablets/galaxy-tab-active/galaxy-tab-active5-pro-enterprise-edition-sm-x358uzgan45/)[[149]](https://www.sunmi.com/en/v3-family/)[[151]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/en-us/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/eda10a/documents/sps-ppr-scanpal-eda10a-tablet-data-sheet-en-us.pdf).

Three findings matter most for product and regional strategy. First, the category is **converging operationally but not economically**. Android SmartPOS devices from PAX, Ingenico, Verifone, and Worldline now run business apps in addition to payment acceptance, while SaaS-led tablet deployments increasingly attach certified readers, docks, barcode engines, and device-management layers [[1]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/main-navigation/solutions/merchants/solutions-and-services/terminals/android-smartpos)[[41]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2023-03/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM%20DX8000_ING_230210.pdf)[[81]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2025-10/INGENICO-Brochure-AXIUM-SEPT25.pdf)[[82]](https://ingenico.com/us-en/products-services/payment-terminals/axium-android)[[127]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/android-smart-pos)[[130]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/top-navigation/media-relations/press-release/pr-2025_06_11_01)[[131]](https://worldline.com/en-gb/home/top-navigation/media-relations/press-release/pr-2025_11_25_01). But that convergence does **not** erase the difference in buyer economics: payment-first devices are often subsidized to acquire processing volume, while SaaS-first tablets are justified by labor efficiency, workflow fit, and software attach [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market)[[73]](https://www.clover.com/pos-systems)[[90]](https://www.deliverect.com/en-us/blog/pos-systems/toast-pos-everything-you-need-to-know)[[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market).

Second, **regional deployment logic is structurally different**. **North America** is the deepest market for vertically integrated SMB commerce stacks, with strong visible ecosystems around Clover, Square, Toast, and Shopify [[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[45]](https://squareup.com/us/en/about)[[46]](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nyse-tost/toast/annual-reports/nyse-tost-2025-10K-25670997.pdf)[[48]](https://s27.q4cdn.com/572064924/files/doc_financials/2025/q4/Investor-Overview-Deck-Q4-2025.pdf)[[64]](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TOST/8-k-toast-inc-reports-material-event-2569dedba178.html). **Japan and Korea** are shaped more by local payment rails, wallet standards, telco/acquirer bundles, and dealer/VAN-controlled installation than by open retail hardware channels [[53]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-pos-terminals-market)[[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market)[[71]](https://en.komoju.com/blog/payment-method/cashless-shift/)[[83]](https://www.meti.go.jp/english/press/2025/0331_001.html)[[196]](https://www.kisvan.co.kr/sub03_04_01.html)[[197]](https://www.kisvan.co.kr/sub01_02_01.html)[[209]](https://www.paycas.jp/paycas-mobile). **Southeast Asia** is more QR/wallet-led and more price-sensitive, which favors low-cost Android endpoints and hybrid QR/card workflows rather than pure card-terminal replacement [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market)[[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf). **South America**, especially **Brazil**, is driven by fintech-acquirer distribution, subsidy economics, contactless migration, and Pix convergence, producing unusually low upfront hardware pricing for merchants [[44]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-america-pos-terminal-market)[[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/)[[178]](https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/d1c190f7-52c1-4663-aa9f-0e0141aa5e3c/8c7bedf1-6e4b-cac1-e2e9-b143c156723b)[[191]](https://www.castlestech.com/brazils-payment-transformation-is-already-here/)[[193]](https://www.ihlservices.com/news/analyst-corner/2026/03/brazil-pos-terminal-market-2026/)[[198]](https://www.mercadopago.com.br/ferramentas-para-vender/maquininhas-point/point-smart)[[201]](https://loja.pagbank.com.br/sem-celular/moderninha-smart-2)[[204]](https://www.ton.com.br/maquininha/t3).

Third, **installed-base visibility is materially fragmented**, so direct unit counts are rare and sometimes misleading. Vendor disclosures report seller counts, merchant counts, shipped devices, or software locations—not always active tablet-style endpoints [[45]](https://squareup.com/us/en/about)[[64]](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TOST/8-k-toast-inc-reports-material-event-2569dedba178.html)[[102]](https://investors.fiserv.com/static-files/33b394b7-c9e0-4d29-8580-8db37c2b8125)[[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/)[[178]](https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/d1c190f7-52c1-4663-aa9f-0e0141aa5e3c/8c7bedf1-6e4b-cac1-e2e9-b143c156723b). Using merchant/location proxies, platform scale, terminal-penetration signals, and category filters, this report estimates the active installed base of **tablet-style payment/SaaS endpoints**—excluding pure smartphone-only acceptance—at **6.5–12.5 million in North America (base 9.0m)**, **1.8–4.0 million in Japan/Korea (base 2.8m)**, **2.2–5.8 million in Southeast Asia (base 3.8m)**, and **2.8–6.5 million in South America (base 4.5m)** [[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[45]](https://squareup.com/us/en/about)[[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market)[[64]](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TOST/8-k-toast-inc-reports-material-event-2569dedba178.html)[[77]](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250428903740/en/Connected-POS-Terminals-Market-Report-2025-Installed-Base-of-Cellular-POS-Terminals-to-Reach-229-Million-in-2028-mPOS-Terminals-Worldwide-to-Reach-152-Million-Units-by-2028---ResearchAndMarkets.com)[[83]](https://www.meti.go.jp/english/press/2025/0331_001.html)[[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[167]](https://ised-isde.canada.ca/site/sme-research-statistics/en/key-small-business-statistics/key-small-business-statistics-2024)[[170]](https://www.ihlservices.com/product/mobile-pos-mpos-market/)[[175]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-payments-market)[[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf)[[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/)[[178]](https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/d1c190f7-52c1-4663-aa9f-0e0141aa5e3c/8c7bedf1-6e4b-cac1-e2e9-b143c156723b). These are **reasoned estimates**, not census totals; confidence is highest in North America and weakest in Southeast Asia and South America, where QR-led acceptance and subsidy-led distribution blur the count of physical devices [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market)[[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market)[[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[101]](https://www.nmi.com/developers/processors-devices/)[[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/).

---

## Scope, Definitions, Assumptions, and Method

### Assumptions box

**Included**
- Touchscreen-first business devices used for **payments**, **POS**, or adjacent **SaaS workflows** such as field service, healthcare, logistics, and inventory [[1]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/main-navigation/solutions/merchants/solutions-and-services/terminals/android-smartpos)[[4]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware)[[7]](https://www.honeywell.com/us/en/news/featured-stories/2021/02/how-field-operations-use-enterprise-rugged-tablets-to-gain-productivity)[[9]](https://www.zebra.com/content/dam/zebra_dam/en/spec-sheets/et60-et65-spec-sheet-en-us.pdf)[[10]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/choosing-your-pos-devices-for-multi-location-retail)[[12]](https://www.samsung.com/us/business/mobile/tablets/galaxy-tab-active/explore/).
- Both **integrated payment devices** and **general-purpose tablets used with POS/SaaS software**, where those tablets are clearly part of merchant workflows [[10]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/choosing-your-pos-devices-for-multi-location-retail)[[11]](https://www.shopify.com/pos/ipad-pos)[[14]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/shopify-pos-terminal)[[16]](https://hardware.shopify.com/pages/poshub)[[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html).
- Both **countertop** and **mobile** form factors, including some 5–7 inch smart terminals when they function as tablet-like business endpoints [[2]](https://ingenico.com/us-en/products-services/payment-terminals/axium-android/axium-ex6000)[[18]](https://cld-stage-merch.fiserv.com/en-uk/taking-payments/compare-clover/)[[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare).

**Excluded**
- Pure smartphone-only payment acceptance unless directly relevant as a substitution threat or comparison point [[101]](https://www.nmi.com/developers/processors-devices/)[[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/).
- Legacy keypad-dominant terminals that are not touchscreen-first, except where needed to explain migration toward SmartPOS [[1]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/main-navigation/solutions/merchants/solutions-and-services/terminals/android-smartpos)[[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market).
- Deep teardown-level component analysis not necessary for product/market assessment.

### Method

This report uses a **triangulation method**:
1. **Official vendor pages and datasheets** for model names, specifications, accessories, operating systems, lifecycle status, and images [[2]](https://ingenico.com/us-en/products-services/payment-terminals/axium-android/axium-ex6000)[[14]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/shopify-pos-terminal)[[15]](https://www.elotouch.com/pos-terminals-elopos-z20.html)[[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)[[41]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2023-03/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM%20DX8000_ING_230210.pdf)[[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html)[[144]](https://www.elotouch.com/mobile-computers-elo-pay-m100-mobile-pos-tablet.html)[[151]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/en-us/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/eda10a/documents/sps-ppr-scanpal-eda10a-tablet-data-sheet-en-us.pdf)[[147]](https://www.samsung.com/ca/business/tablets/galaxy-tab-active/galaxy-tab-active5-pro-enterprise-edition-sm-x358uzgan45/)[[149]](https://www.sunmi.com/en/v3-family/).
2. **Public filings, investor materials, and earnings commentary** for ecosystem scale, location counts, merchant counts, and strategic direction [[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/)[[45]](https://squareup.com/us/en/about)[[46]](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nyse-tost/toast/annual-reports/nyse-tost-2025-10K-25670997.pdf)[[48]](https://s27.q4cdn.com/572064924/files/doc_financials/2025/q4/Investor-Overview-Deck-Q4-2025.pdf)[[49]](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1594805/000159480526000006/exhibit991pressreleaseq420.htm)[[64]](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TOST/8-k-toast-inc-reports-material-event-2569dedba178.html)[[102]](https://investors.fiserv.com/static-files/33b394b7-c9e0-4d29-8580-8db37c2b8125)[[108]](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250219799274/en/Toast-Announces-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2024-Financial-Results)[[109]](https://block.xyz/documents/block-investor-day-2025-financial-outlook.pdf)[[133]](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/798354/000079835425000168/fiq325earningsrelease.htm)[[137]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/toast-announces-fourth-quarter-full-210500465.html)[[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/)[[178]](https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/d1c190f7-52c1-4663-aa9f-0e0141aa5e3c/8c7bedf1-6e4b-cac1-e2e9-b143c156723b).
3. **Government, trade-association, and payments-body statistics** for merchant-market structure and cashless adoption [[83]](https://www.meti.go.jp/english/press/2025/0331_001.html)[[167]](https://ised-isde.canada.ca/site/sme-research-statistics/en/key-small-business-statistics/key-small-business-statistics-2024)[[168]](https://www.census.gov/newsroom/press-releases/2025/2023-county-business-patterns.html)[[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf)[[180]](https://20392958.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/20392958/AA%20US%20White%20Papers/Pix%20White%20Paper%20By%20Matera.pdf)[[181]](https://paymentscmi.com/insights/pix-in-brazil-latest-statistics-central-bank/).
4. **Recent market-report summaries** for terminal mix, mobile share, and regional channel dynamics, used as triangulation rather than as stand-alone proof [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market)[[53]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-pos-terminals-market)[[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market)[[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[128]](https://www.congruencemarketinsights.com/report/mobile-pos-terminals-market)[[174]](https://market.us/report/global-mobile-pos-systems-market/)[[175]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-payments-market)[[193]](https://www.ihlservices.com/news/analyst-corner/2026/03/brazil-pos-terminal-market-2026/).

### Confidence-rating framework

- **High**: official vendor, filing, or official public statistic directly supports the claim.
- **Medium**: multiple credible sources point in the same direction, but category filtering is needed.
- **Low**: estimate depends heavily on proxies, reseller pricing, or ambiguous boundaries between terminals, tablets, and software-only acceptance [[102]](https://investors.fiserv.com/static-files/33b394b7-c9e0-4d29-8580-8db37c2b8125)[[116]](https://www.precisionreports.co/market-reports/tablet-pos-systems-market-605319)[[117]](https://www.globalgrowthinsights.com/market-reports/tablet-pos-systems-market-110563)[[185]](https://clearingpost.com/insights/pix-captures-one-third-brazil-pos-payments-2025-worldpay/).

## Category Framework: What Qualifies as a Tablet-Style Payment or SaaS Device

### Device taxonomy

| Device class | Definition | Typical examples | Why buyers choose it | Key trade-off |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Dedicated payment tablets / SmartPOS** | Android smart terminals with integrated EMV/NFC, often printer/scanner/camera | PAX A920Pro, Ingenico DX8000, Verifone Victa Mobile, Clover Flex, PayCAS Mobile [[2]](https://ingenico.com/us-en/products-services/payment-terminals/axium-android/axium-ex6000)[[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[40]](https://connect.verifone.com/en/us/verifone-victa/verifone-victa-mobile)[[41]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2023-03/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM%20DX8000_ING_230210.pdf)[[85]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/a920pro)[[209]](https://www.paycas.jp/paycas-mobile) | Certified payments, rapid rollout, app-capable payment estate | Less flexible than open tablets |
| **Tablet POS terminals** | Merchant-facing/customer-facing POS systems that integrate or pair with payment hardware | Clover Mini / Station, Square Register / Stand, Toast Flex, Shopify POS Terminal + tablet, Elo Pay M100 [[5]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/pos-terminal-countertop-kit-for-usb-c-tablets)[[14]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/shopify-pos-terminal)[[15]](https://www.elotouch.com/pos-terminals-elopos-z20.html)[[17]](https://support.toasttab.com/en/article/Toast-Hardware-Hub)[[21]](https://d2c9w5yn32a2ju.cloudfront.net/knowledgebase/Toast-Hardware_Specs_DIGITAL.pdf)[[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)[[35]](https://support.toasttab.com/en/article/What-are-the-dimensions-of-a-Toast-Go-2)[[144]](https://www.elotouch.com/mobile-computers-elo-pay-m100-mobile-pos-tablet.html) | Better checkout UX, accessories, customer display, fixed + mobile workflows | Higher bundle complexity or vendor lock-in |
| **Enterprise rugged tablets used with SaaS** | Rugged tablets for field/logistics/retail/healthcare workflows, sometimes payment-ready | Zebra ET40/45, Zebra ET6x, Honeywell EDA10A, Honeywell RT10A, Samsung Tab Active5 Pro [[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html)[[146]](https://www.zebra.com/content/dam/support-dam/en/documentation/unrestricted/guide/product/et6x-prg-en.pdf)[[151]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/en-us/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/eda10a/documents/sps-ppr-scanpal-eda10a-tablet-data-sheet-en-us.pdf)[[157]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/pt-br/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/rt10a/documents/sps-ppr-rt10a-rugged-tablet-data-sheet-en.pdf)[[147]](https://www.samsung.com/ca/business/tablets/galaxy-tab-active/galaxy-tab-active5-pro-enterprise-edition-sm-x358uzgan45/) | Ruggedness, long lifecycle, scanning, vehicle docks, hot-swap batteries | Payment is often external or secondary |
| **Consumer tablets as business endpoints** | iPads/Android tablets running POS or SaaS with external reader/terminal | Shopify POS on iPad/Android tablet, Square Stand/Kiosk, restaurant and retail iPad setups [[10]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/choosing-your-pos-devices-for-multi-location-retail)[[11]](https://www.shopify.com/pos/ipad-pos)[[16]](https://hardware.shopify.com/pages/poshub)[[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)[[76]](https://www.shopify.com/pos/hardware) | Low entry cost, flexible software, easy omnichannel tie-in | More components, more OS/peripheral dependency |

### Inclusion logic

A device qualifies here if it is:
- **Touchscreen-first**,
- Used directly in **merchant, staff, or customer workflows**,
- Deployed for **payments or business SaaS**, and
- Supported by a real **peripheral, app, or payment ecosystem** [[1]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/main-navigation/solutions/merchants/solutions-and-services/terminals/android-smartpos)[[4]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware)[[10]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/choosing-your-pos-devices-for-multi-location-retail)[[16]](https://hardware.shopify.com/pages/poshub)[[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html)[[101]](https://www.nmi.com/developers/processors-devices/).

### Why the segmentation matters commercially

Treating this as one hardware market obscures the actual buying decision. A **PAX A920Pro** and a **Zebra ET45** can both be used in checkout-adjacent workflows, but one is typically selected through a payment channel for payment certification and estate control, while the other is selected through enterprise IT for scanning, ruggedness, and multi-app workflow support [[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html)[[85]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/a920pro). Likewise, a **Shopify iPad setup** and a **Clover Mini** can both run a store counter, but the first assumes the tablet is the main computer and payments are modular, while the second assumes payment is embedded and the business workflow is layered onto that certified base [[10]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/choosing-your-pos-devices-for-multi-location-retail)[[14]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/shopify-pos-terminal)[[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[76]](https://www.shopify.com/pos/hardware).

### What the landscape means

The vendor split reflects **who owns the merchant relationship**. **Clover, Square, Toast, and Shopify** matter most when the merchant or chain operator is buying a full commerce platform [[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[45]](https://squareup.com/us/en/about)[[46]](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nyse-tost/toast/annual-reports/nyse-tost-2025-10K-25670997.pdf)[[48]](https://s27.q4cdn.com/572064924/files/doc_financials/2025/q4/Investor-Overview-Deck-Q4-2025.pdf). **PAX, Ingenico, and Verifone** matter most when the device decision is made by a **bank, acquirer, PSP, or distributor**, because those channels prioritize certification, key injection, estate control, and regional payment compatibility [[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/)[[41]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2023-03/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM%20DX8000_ING_230210.pdf)[[47]](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/01/09/3007276/0/en/verifone-unveils-new-payment-device-portfolio-and-innovations-to-revolutionize-commerce.html)[[88]](https://www.pax.us/products/). **Zebra, Honeywell, Samsung, and Elo** matter when the device’s main job is not only checkout, but also mobility, scanning, ruggedness, or fixed/mobile workflow conversion [[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html)[[144]](https://www.elotouch.com/mobile-computers-elo-pay-m100-mobile-pos-tablet.html)[[151]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/en-us/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/eda10a/documents/sps-ppr-scanpal-eda10a-tablet-data-sheet-en-us.pdf)[[147]](https://www.samsung.com/ca/business/tablets/galaxy-tab-active/galaxy-tab-active5-pro-enterprise-edition-sm-x358uzgan45/).

### Recent market-status signals that matter now

- **Clover refresh pressure is real**: first-generation Clover Station, Mobile, Mini, and Flex devices approach **End-of-App-Update on 30 March 2026**, forcing merchants and developers to plan hardware migration [[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs).
- **Clover is still expanding geographically**: announcements show **Flex 4** rolling out across the U.S., Europe, Canada, and the UK, and **Clover Compact** arriving for U.S. semi-integration in 2025 [[132]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/release-notes-announcements).
- **Worldline’s SmartPOS rollout in 2025**—Switzerland in June and UK in November—shows large processors now explicitly positioning Android payment terminals as business-app platforms, not just card devices [[130]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/top-navigation/media-relations/press-release/pr-2025_06_11_01)[[131]](https://worldline.com/en-gb/home/top-navigation/media-relations/press-release/pr-2025_11_25_01).
- **PAX says Japanese A920MAX sales nearly doubled year-on-year**, indicating smart-terminal acceleration in a market often seen as slower-moving [[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/).
- **Fiserv’s TD Bank merchant-processing acquisition in Canada** was explicitly framed as expanding the footprint of the Clover platform, showing that bank-distribution deals can move installed base as much as merchant self-selection [[133]](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/798354/000079835425000168/fiq325earningsrelease.htm).

The causal implication is straightforward: as the hardware becomes more app-capable, **distribution control** matters more. Vendors that already sit in a bank, acquirer, or vertical SaaS channel can attach devices at lower acquisition cost than a stand-alone hardware OEM can [[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/)[[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[133]](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/798354/000079835425000168/fiq325earningsrelease.htm).

### Device findings that matter commercially

1. **Elo Pay M100 is one of the clearest bridge products** between the payment-first and SaaS-first camps. It is a **10.1-inch Android 12 tablet** with **integrated EMV/NFC**, optional **SE4100 1D/2D barcode scanner**, optional LTE, user-replaceable **8000mAh battery**, and a **rotating dock** that converts the device from mobile to fixed POS [[144]](https://www.elotouch.com/mobile-computers-elo-pay-m100-mobile-pos-tablet.html)[[153]](https://docs.elotouch.com/Elo_Pay_M100_Mobile_POS_Tablet_DS.pdf). That combination is unusual because most integrated-payment devices are smaller handhelds, while most 10-inch tablets require external readers.

2. **Square and Shopify still embody the modular tablet model**. Square Stand and Kiosk remain iPad-dependent, and Shopify’s POS Terminal/Countertop Kit is explicitly a paired setup rather than a single integrated slab [[5]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/pos-terminal-countertop-kit-for-usb-c-tablets)[[14]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/shopify-pos-terminal)[[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare). This lowers initial hardware specialization but increases dependency on tablet compatibility, OS support, and the peripheral bill of materials.

3. **PAX, Ingenico, and Verifone increasingly compete on app-capable payment hardware rather than just certification**. PAX emphasizes Android SmartPOS and app distribution through MAXSTORE/PAXSTORE [[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/)[[88]](https://www.pax.us/products/)[[127]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/android-smart-pos); Ingenico’s AXIUM family adds broader screen formats and accessory options [[41]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2023-03/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM%20DX8000_ING_230210.pdf)[[82]](https://ingenico.com/us-en/products-services/payment-terminals/axium-android)[[160]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2024-06/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM_RX9000_ING_240227.pdf); Verifone’s Victa Mobile pushes further toward phone-like form factors with a **6.7-inch screen** and **4GB/32GB** compute profile [[40]](https://connect.verifone.com/en/us/verifone-victa/verifone-victa-mobile)[[47]](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2025/01/09/3007276/0/en/verifone-unveils-new-payment-device-portfolio-and-innovations-to-revolutionize-commerce.html).

4. **Rugged enterprise tablets are usually selected for workflow intensity, not payments**. Zebra, Honeywell, and Samsung add value through scan engines, docks, hot-swap batteries, enterprise device management, and longer support windows [[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html)[[147]](https://www.samsung.com/ca/business/tablets/galaxy-tab-active/galaxy-tab-active5-pro-enterprise-edition-sm-x358uzgan45/)[[151]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/en-us/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/eda10a/documents/sps-ppr-scanpal-eda10a-tablet-data-sheet-en-us.pdf)[[157]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/pt-br/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/rt10a/documents/sps-ppr-rt10a-rugged-tablet-data-sheet-en.pdf). Payment support exists, but it is rarely the primary selection criterion.

## Use-Case Analysis: Payments vs SaaS Workflow Deployments

### Payment-focused vs SaaS-focused device comparison table

| Dimension | Payment-focused tablet-style devices | SaaS-focused tablet deployments |
|---|---|---|
| Typical hardware | Integrated EMV/NFC, often printer/camera/scanner | Larger screen, ruggedness, docks, scanners, external reader options |
| Typical buyer | Acquirer, PSP, or SMB choosing turnkey payments | Retail/restaurant operator, enterprise IT, field ops, omnichannel retailer |
| Peripheral attach rate | Lower in simple setups; higher in counter bundles | Higher: scanners, printers, docks, keyboards, vehicle mounts |
| Software stack | Certified payment kernel + lightweight business apps | Full POS/SaaS workflow + modular payments |
| Replacement cycle | Often driven by certification, security, processor programs | Driven by OS lifecycle, ruggedness, breakage, workflow change |
| Economics | Hardware often subsidized or bundled to pull processing volume | Hardware often merchant-owned; software and peripherals are larger share of TCO |
| Strengths | Faster deployment, compliance, lower setup complexity | Flexibility, broader workflow support, easier vertical customization |
| Weaknesses | More lock-in, less hardware openness | More components, more integration points |
| Evidence | [[1]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/main-navigation/solutions/merchants/solutions-and-services/terminals/android-smartpos)[[2]](https://ingenico.com/us-en/products-services/payment-terminals/axium-android/axium-ex6000)[[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/)[[41]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2023-03/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM%20DX8000_ING_230210.pdf)[[81]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2025-10/INGENICO-Brochure-AXIUM-SEPT25.pdf)[[85]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/a920pro)[[127]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/android-smart-pos) | [[10]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/choosing-your-pos-devices-for-multi-location-retail)[[16]](https://hardware.shopify.com/pages/poshub)[[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html)[[151]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/en-us/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/eda10a/documents/sps-ppr-scanpal-eda10a-tablet-data-sheet-en-us.pdf)[[147]](https://www.samsung.com/ca/business/tablets/galaxy-tab-active/galaxy-tab-active5-pro-enterprise-edition-sm-x358uzgan45/)[[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf) |

### Analysis

The core trade-off is **certified-payment simplicity versus workflow flexibility**. Worldline explicitly describes SmartPOS as a way to load business applications directly onto an Android payment terminal, turning the device into a multi-purpose merchant tool while retaining payment acceptance [[1]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/main-navigation/solutions/merchants/solutions-and-services/terminals/android-smartpos)[[130]](https://worldline.com/en-ch/home/top-navigation/media-relations/press-release/pr-2025_06_11_01)[[131]](https://worldline.com/en-gb/home/top-navigation/media-relations/press-release/pr-2025_11_25_01). PAX and Ingenico make the same move: the device begins as a secure payment endpoint, then grows upward into loyalty, order-taking, stock, or service workflows [[41]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2023-03/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM%20DX8000_ING_230210.pdf)[[85]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/a920pro)[[127]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/android-smart-pos).

The causal mechanism is important. Once a payment terminal can run apps, the merchant avoids buying and managing a separate tablet for basic operational tasks. That reduces device count and onboarding time, which is especially valuable in bank- or acquirer-led deployments. But the merchant also accepts tighter coupling to the processor’s hardware approvals, app distribution model, and lifecycle timetable [[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/)[[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[41]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2023-03/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM%20DX8000_ING_230210.pdf).

In the **SaaS-first** model—Square Stand, Shopify POS on iPad/Android, Zebra ET-series, Honeywell tablets, Samsung Tab Active—the device is the **primary business computer**. Payment becomes one module among many [[10]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/choosing-your-pos-devices-for-multi-location-retail)[[16]](https://hardware.shopify.com/pages/poshub)[[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)[[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html)[[151]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/en-us/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/eda10a/documents/sps-ppr-scanpal-eda10a-tablet-data-sheet-en-us.pdf)[[147]](https://www.samsung.com/ca/business/tablets/galaxy-tab-active/galaxy-tab-active5-pro-enterprise-edition-sm-x358uzgan45/). That architecture wins when the workflow is richer than checkout alone: tableside ordering, back-of-house inventory, healthcare intake, proof-of-delivery, appointment scheduling, or field service documentation. The price of that flexibility is higher integration burden: more peripherals, more OS compatibility issues, and more responsibility for device management outside the payment processor’s standard stack [[10]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/choosing-your-pos-devices-for-multi-location-retail)[[76]](https://www.shopify.com/pos/hardware)[[101]](https://www.nmi.com/developers/processors-devices/).

### Deployment mechanisms by vertical

**Retail POS.** Retail checkouts value larger screens, customer-facing displays, inventory sync, barcode support, and accessory attachment. That is why Square Register, Clover Mini/Station, Shopify tablet setups, Elo Pay M100, and SUNMI desktop-class systems are structurally strong in this use case [[15]](https://www.elotouch.com/pos-terminals-elopos-z20.html)[[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)[[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[76]](https://www.shopify.com/pos/hardware)[[161]](https://file.cdn.sunmi.com/newebsite/downloads/specs/en/T2s.pdf). The hardware job is not only payment capture; it is SKU lookup, staff workflow, and customer confirmation.

**Restaurants.** Restaurant operators overweight handheld mobility, order-entry ergonomics, modifier handling, and kitchen synchronization. This is why **Toast Go**, **Clover Flex**, and **Square Handheld** matter more than generic rugged tablets in tableside deployments [[19]](https://pos.toasttab.com/ie/products/handheld-pos)[[29]](https://squareup.com/us/en/press/square-handheld)[[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[138]](https://s28.q4cdn.com/141746709/files/doc_financials/2025/q2/Q2-25-Consolidated-Prepared-Remarks.pdf). Toast’s explicit **offline continuity** matters here because restaurant service cannot stop when connectivity dips [[46]](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nyse-tost/toast/annual-reports/nyse-tost-2025-10K-25670997.pdf)[[104]](https://materials.proxyvote.com/Approved/888787/20250415/AR_604939.PDF).

**Line-busting and mobile checkout.** Handheld payment terminals and compact tablets win because they collapse queue reduction, order entry, and payment into one operator motion. Square Handheld, Clover Flex, PAX A920-class devices, and Verifone Victa Mobile fit this pattern [[29]](https://squareup.com/us/en/press/square-handheld)[[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[40]](https://connect.verifone.com/en/us/verifone-victa/verifone-victa-mobile)[[85]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/a920pro). The mechanism is labor efficiency: fewer handoffs and less return to a fixed counter.

**Self-service and kiosk.** Larger displays, stable mounts, and customer-facing UI matter more than pure portability. Clover Kiosk, Square Kiosk, Toast Kiosk, Ingenico RX-class, and SUNMI/Elo desktop systems are the most relevant comparison set [[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)[[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[79]](https://pos.toasttab.com/hardware)[[81]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2025-10/INGENICO-Brochure-AXIUM-SEPT25.pdf)[[160]](https://ingenico.com/sites/default/files/resource-document/2024-06/USA-CAN_DATASHEET_AXIUM_RX9000_ING_240227.pdf)[[161]](https://file.cdn.sunmi.com/newebsite/downloads/specs/en/T2s.pdf).

**Field service, logistics, and healthcare.** Zebra, Honeywell, and Samsung dominate because these environments punish fragile hardware. Scan engines, vehicle docks, hand straps, hot-swappable batteries, and long support windows affect uptime more than integrated EMV does [[74]](https://www.zebra.com/us/en/products/spec-sheets/tablets/et40-et45.html)[[147]](https://www.samsung.com/ca/business/tablets/galaxy-tab-active/galaxy-tab-active5-pro-enterprise-edition-sm-x358uzgan45/)[[151]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/en-us/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/eda10a/documents/sps-ppr-scanpal-eda10a-tablet-data-sheet-en-us.pdf)[[157]](https://prod-edam.honeywell.com/content/dam/honeywell-edam/sps/ppr/pt-br/public/products/mobile-computers/tablets/rt10a/documents/sps-ppr-rt10a-rugged-tablet-data-sheet-en.pdf). Payment may still be added, but only when it does not compromise field workflow.

## Regional Market Analysis: North America

### Market penetration

North America is the **best-documented region** for this category because the leading platforms disclose meaningful ecosystem scale. **Clover says 4M+ devices shipped** and **$337B+ annualized processing volume** [[37]](https://www.clover.com/). **Square says more than 4 million sellers run on Square**, it processed **$209.6B in payments in 2023**, and it serves **8 countries** [[45]](https://squareup.com/us/en/about). **Toast ended 2025 at approximately 164,000 locations** [[64]](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TOST/8-k-toast-inc-reports-material-event-2569dedba178.html)[[137]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/toast-announces-fourth-quarter-full-210500465.html). These figures are not additive—seller counts, shipped devices, and locations measure different things—but together they establish a much denser installed base than in the other three regions [[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[45]](https://squareup.com/us/en/about)[[64]](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TOST/8-k-toast-inc-reports-material-event-2569dedba178.html).

Market summaries also consistently place North America as the largest regional revenue block in mobile POS/tablet POS, with shares in the **high-30% range** across summaries [[128]](https://www.congruencemarketinsights.com/report/mobile-pos-terminals-market)[[174]](https://market.us/report/global-mobile-pos-systems-market/). That matters less as a unit count than as confirmation that hardware spending, software attachment, and replacement activity are concentrated here.

### Common usage scenarios

North America’s mix is driven by **software-led merchant operating platforms** rather than by bank-distributed generic terminals. The most common patterns are:
- **Retail countertop POS**: Square Register/Stand, Clover Mini/Station, Shopify tablet + terminal [[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)[[76]](https://www.shopify.com/pos/hardware)
- **Restaurant handheld + kitchen stack**: Toast Go/Flex/KDS, Clover Flex, Square Handheld [[19]](https://pos.toasttab.com/ie/products/handheld-pos)[[21]](https://d2c9w5yn32a2ju.cloudfront.net/knowledgebase/Toast-Hardware_Specs_DIGITAL.pdf)[[29]](https://squareup.com/us/en/press/square-handheld)[[37]](https://www.clover.com/)
- **Self-service kiosk**: Clover Kiosk, Square Kiosk, Toast Kiosk [[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)[[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[79]](https://pos.toasttab.com/hardware)
- **Omnichannel retail**: Shopify and Square’s tablet-led deployments are strongest here because software, online/offline sync, and consumer tablet familiarity reduce adoption friction [[34]](https://www.shopify.com/blog/pos-companies)[[48]](https://s27.q4cdn.com/572064924/files/doc_financials/2025/q4/Investor-Overview-Deck-Q4-2025.pdf)

### Typical price ranges

Observed North American list or channel prices:
- **Square Reader**: **$59** [[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)
- **Square Stand**: **$149** [[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)
- **Square Terminal**: **$299** [[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)
- **Square Handheld**: **$399** [[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)
- **Shopify POS Terminal**: **$349** [[14]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/shopify-pos-terminal)
- **Shopify POS Terminal Countertop Kit**: **$459** [[5]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/pos-terminal-countertop-kit-for-usb-c-tablets)
- **Square Register (2nd gen)**: **$899** on the live hardware page; an older Square-owned explainer cited **$799**, showing that vendor-owned pricing references can drift over time [[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare)[[171]](https://squareup.com/us/en/the-bottom-line/operating-your-business/pos-system-cost)
- **Clover Flex / Mini / Station Solo / Station Duo**: approximately **$749 / $949 / $1,799 / $2,099** in reseller/channel evidence, which is useful directionally but is not official MSRP [[28]](https://dharmamerchantservices.com/services/point-of-sale/clover-point-sale/)
- **Toast Starter Kit**: can be offered at **$0 upfront** under processing-tied plans; third-party bundle references place broader restaurant hardware bundles in the several-hundred to low-thousand-dollar range [[90]](https://www.deliverect.com/en-us/blog/pos-systems/toast-pos-everything-you-need-to-know)[[94]](https://koronapos.com/blog/toast-pos-cost-calculator/)

The mechanism behind visible North American pricing is that providers can expose hardware price separately because they expect recurring monetization from payments and software afterward. Toast’s **$0 upfront** option makes that explicit: the economic decision is not hardware-only capex, but lifetime payment and SaaS revenue [[90]](https://www.deliverect.com/en-us/blog/pos-systems/toast-pos-everything-you-need-to-know).

### Estimated installed base

**Estimate (active tablet-style devices, North America):**
- **Low:** 6.5 million
- **Base:** 9.0 million
- **High:** 12.5 million
- **Confidence:** **Medium**

**Why this range is defensible.** Canada alone had **1,095,251 employer businesses** as of December 2023, with more than three in four having **1–9 employees**—exactly the segment that disproportionately adopts lower-capex tablet POS and mobile checkout tools [[167]](https://ised-isde.canada.ca/site/sme-research-statistics/en/key-small-business-statistics/key-small-business-statistics-2024). The U.S. Census materials confirm the establishment-based framework needed for a larger merchant base, though the accessible excerpt here does not provide the national count [[168]](https://www.census.gov/newsroom/press-releases/2025/2023-county-business-patterns.html)[[169]](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/arts.html). Against that backdrop, the platform indicators—**Clover 4M+ devices shipped**, **Square 4M+ sellers**, **Toast ~164k locations**—make a 9.0 million active device base plausible without forcing aggressive assumptions [[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[45]](https://squareup.com/us/en/about)[[64]](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TOST/8-k-toast-inc-reports-material-event-2569dedba178.html). The estimate excludes pure smartphone-only acceptance but includes countertop tablet POS, handheld SmartPOS, and clearly deployed business tablets used in checkout-adjacent SaaS workflows [[101]](https://www.nmi.com/developers/processors-devices/)[[169]](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/arts.html)[[170]](https://www.ihlservices.com/product/mobile-pos-mpos-market/).

## Regional Market Analysis: Japan and Korea

### Japan

#### Market penetration

Japan’s strongest anchor is policy-linked cashless adoption. METI says the **cashless payment ratio reached 42.8% in 2024**, surpassing the national **40% target**, with **141.0 trillion yen** in cashless payments, including **116.9 trillion yen on credit cards** [[83]](https://www.meti.go.jp/english/press/2025/0331_001.html). KOMOJU reported a **39.3%** cashless ratio in 2023, up from **36.0%** in 2022, and **75.8 million** QR/barcode users in 2023 [[71]](https://en.komoju.com/blog/payment-method/cashless-shift/). Another market summary says **merchant acceptance of digital wallets exceeds 3 million terminals nationwide** [[175]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-payments-market). That 3 million is not a tablet-style device count, but it is a useful upper-bound ecosystem signal.

#### Common usage scenarios

Japan’s device mix reflects local rail complexity:
- **QR- and wallet-heavy SMB acceptance**, especially where merchants avoid large upfront terminal capex [[71]](https://en.komoju.com/blog/payment-method/cashless-shift/)
- **Tourism and transit-linked retail**, where contactless and multilingual wallet acceptance matter [[175]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-payments-market)
- **Healthcare/admin flows**, where identity and payment can converge on one screen [[53]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-pos-terminals-market)[[175]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-payments-market)
- **Portable merchant scenarios** such as food trucks and temporary retail, evidenced by PayCAS Mobile case materials [[209]](https://www.paycas.jp/paycas-mobile)

#### Leading devices and channels

The clearest current bundled example is **PayCAS Mobile**, a SoftBank-linked solution built on **PAX A920 / A920MAX-class hardware**, with **Android 10**, a **6.5-inch screen**, integrated printer, **FeliCa support**, and app distribution via **PayCAS DX Store** [[209]](https://www.paycas.jp/paycas-mobile)[[210]](https://www.paycas.jp/peripheral). PAX separately says Japanese **A920MAX sales nearly doubled year-on-year** as banks and PSPs accelerated deployment [[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/). The causal point is that Japan is not adopting tablet-style payment hardware mainly through open device retail; it is adopting it through **service bundles aligned to local payment rails**.

#### Typical price ranges

Public local device pricing is weak in the captured evidence, so the most defensible view is:
- **Observed local device MSRP:** not captured publicly for PayCAS Mobile [[209]](https://www.paycas.jp/paycas-mobile)[[210]](https://www.paycas.jp/peripheral)
- **Global hardware proxies:** PAX A920-class around **$375–$393** from non-Japan resellers [[211]](https://shop.emerchantauthority.com/products/pax-a920)[[212]](https://www.cdw.com/product/pax-technology-a920-android-smart-pos-terminal/6922530)[[213]](https://posguys.com/payment-terminals_37/PAX-Technology-A920_4119/)
- **Open-market Android smart POS proxy:** SUNMI V3 Plus roughly **$449–$479** [[208]](https://allstarterminals.com/collections/sunmi-payment-solutions)

That does **not** mean Japanese merchants actually pay those prices directly. It means public retail pricing is not the main route to market; acquiring and service bundles are [[209]](https://www.paycas.jp/paycas-mobile)[[210]](https://www.paycas.jp/peripheral).

### Korea

#### Market penetration

South Korea combines **advanced digital-payment behavior** with a still-incomplete hardware refresh. One current summary says the country has **2.9 million merchant locations**, and **Zero Pay is accepted at more than 2 million businesses** [[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market). Yet the same source says only **about 10% of stores had compliant NFC readers by March 2025**, and a basic upgrade cost about **KRW 200,000 (~USD 138)** [[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market). This is one of the clearest examples in the report where demand-side readiness and supply-side estate modernization diverge.

The mechanism is channel structure. Korea remains heavily **VAN/dealer-led**, with provider-controlled registration, regional dealer assignment, site visits, and installation processes [[196]](https://www.kisvan.co.kr/sub03_04_01.html)[[197]](https://www.kisvan.co.kr/sub01_02_01.html)[[214]](https://www.kovan.com/). That slows the speed at which a technically desirable terminal refresh becomes a visible open-market hardware opportunity.

#### Common usage scenarios

Korean deployments cluster around:
- **Chain retail smart checkout**
- **Convenience and department-store omnichannel**
- **Last-mile delivery / curbside / in-aisle portable devices**
- **Duty-free and hospitality**
- **Healthcare authentication/payment workflows** [[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market)

#### Typical price ranges

Captured public pricing is thin. The strongest visible anchor is:
- **Basic NFC-capable terminal upgrade cost:** about **KRW 200,000 (~USD 138)** [[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market)

That is not a full smart tablet POS price; it is best read as a **baseline modernization cost**. Higher-end Android smart terminals appear to move mainly through dealer/VAN bundles rather than public e-commerce storefronts [[196]](https://www.kisvan.co.kr/sub03_04_01.html)[[197]](https://www.kisvan.co.kr/sub01_02_01.html)[[214]](https://www.kovan.com/).

### Japan + Korea installed-base estimate

**Estimate (active tablet-style devices, Japan + Korea):**
- **Low:** 1.8 million
- **Base:** 2.8 million
- **High:** 4.0 million
- **Confidence:** **Medium-low**

**Why this range is defensible.** Japan’s **3 million+ wallet-accepting terminals** provide a clear upper-bound ecosystem indicator, but many are not tablet-style devices [[175]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-payments-market). Korea’s **2.9 million merchant locations** establish a large addressable market, but the **~10% NFC-compliant-reader** figure shows how incomplete the modern hardware estate still is [[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market). The base estimate assumes meaningful smart-terminal and tablet deployment in urban retail, hospitality, transport, and services, while discounting heavily for QR-only acceptance, legacy readers, and non-tablet hardware [[53]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-pos-terminals-market)[[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market)[[71]](https://en.komoju.com/blog/payment-method/cashless-shift/)[[83]](https://www.meti.go.jp/english/press/2025/0331_001.html)[[175]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-payments-market)[[209]](https://www.paycas.jp/paycas-mobile).

## Regional Market Analysis: Southeast Asia

### Market penetration

Southeast Asia is the most **hardware-growth-oriented** region in this study, but not the most hardware-dependent. A current market summary values the regional POS terminal market at **USD 4.96 billion in 2025**, rising to **USD 11.8 billion by 2031**, a **15.55% CAGR** [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market). The same source says **contactless accounted for 56.97% of revenue in 2025** and **mobile/portable readers 46.25%** [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market). IDC/2C2P add that digital payments already account for **87% of e-commerce payments in 2023**, rising to **94% by 2028** across six major markets [[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf).

Those figures show strong payment digitization, but they do not translate one-for-one into tablet hardware counts. In Southeast Asia, merchants can digitize payments through QR and wallets without buying an expensive certified card terminal. That weakens the historical link between “more cashless payments” and “more dedicated POS tablets” [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market)[[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf).

### Common usage scenarios

The region’s dominant use cases differ from North America:
- **SME and micro-merchant cash-register replacement** using low-cost Android tablets or smart terminals
- **Kiosks, pop-ups, food trucks, and mobile merchants**
- **QR-led and wallet-led acceptance**
- **Omnichannel growth among larger merchants** [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market)[[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf)

A useful scale signal is that **DealPOS and Qasir together serve 500,000 merchants in Indonesia** [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market). That does not mean 500,000 tablet endpoints, but it does show that SaaS-led merchant operating stacks have reached material scale in the largest Southeast Asian market.

### Why adoption looks different here

The most important causal difference is payment rail mix. IDC/2C2P show very large local-wallet and domestic-payment user bases, including **125.1 million mobile-wallet users in Indonesia**, **46.2 million in the Philippines**, and **68.5 million PromptPay users in Thailand** [[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf). For many merchants, the first digital acceptance upgrade is therefore **QR/wallet capability**, not a full card-centric POS replacement. That favors:
- lower-cost Android endpoints,
- shared merchant devices,
- tablet-plus-QR workflows,
- and software-led SMB tools that can run on general tablets or low-cost smart terminals [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market)[[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf).

### Typical price ranges

The price structure is bifurcated:
- **Open-market Android handheld SmartPOS proxies**: PAX A920-class at roughly **$375–$455**, SUNMI V3 family around **$439–$479** [[61]](https://paybotx.company.site/Pax-A920-Max-p796101115)[[208]](https://allstarterminals.com/collections/sunmi-payment-solutions)[[211]](https://shop.emerchantauthority.com/products/pax-a920)[[212]](https://www.cdw.com/product/pax-technology-a920-android-smart-pos-terminal/6922530)
- **Broader “modern contactless-enabled terminal” / fuller-solution figures**: **$1,500–$3,000 upfront plus $500–$1,000 yearly fees** in one regional summary [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market)

These are not contradictory; they refer to different commercial packages. The lower figures resemble **device-only handheld SmartPOS**. The higher figures likely reflect **full business systems or enterprise bundles** including software, service, and installation [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market).

### Estimated installed base

**Estimate (active tablet-style devices, Southeast Asia):**
- **Low:** 2.2 million
- **Base:** 3.8 million
- **High:** 5.8 million
- **Confidence:** **Low-medium**

**Why this range is defensible.** The market clearly has strong SME digitization and very high growth, but physical-device counting is difficult because many merchants accept QR/wallet payments without deploying a high-end tablet POS [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market)[[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf). The base case assumes millions of active Android smartPOS and tablet endpoints region-wide, with **Indonesia the largest contributor**, but discounts heavily for QR-only and software-only acceptance patterns [[43]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/southeast-asia-pos-terminal-market).

## Regional Market Analysis: South America

### Market penetration

South America’s most important current story is **Brazil**, where smart terminals have become a core merchant-acquisition tool. Brazil’s POS terminal market is estimated at **USD 6.45 billion in 2025**, rising to **USD 9.16 billion by 2031** [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market). **Mobile and portable solutions accounted for 53.97% of Brazil’s POS terminal market size in 2025** and are projected to post the fastest CAGR through 2031 [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market). A broader South America summary says **mobile/portable devices accounted for 63.72% of regional shipments in 2024** and that Brazil represented **34.72% of South American POS terminal revenue in 2024** [[44]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-america-pos-terminal-market).

Brazil also has unusually strong contactless signals. IHL says the country has **68% contactless terminal penetration across the installed POS base** [[193]](https://www.ihlservices.com/news/analyst-corner/2026/03/brazil-pos-terminal-market-2026/), while Mordor says **contactless card payments reached 67.2% of in-person card transactions by December 2024** [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market). Those are different measures, but directionally they tell the same story: merchants and consumers are both operating in a highly contactless environment [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[193]](https://www.ihlservices.com/news/analyst-corner/2026/03/brazil-pos-terminal-market-2026/).

### Common usage scenarios

Brazilian and regional use cases extend beyond fixed retail counters:
- **Micro-merchants and SMB checkout**
- **Restaurants and tableside service**
- **Transportation and ticketing**
- **Logistics and proof-of-delivery**
- **Healthcare front desk and kiosk**
- **Professional services and mobile billing** [[44]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-america-pos-terminal-market)[[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[191]](https://www.castlestech.com/brazils-payment-transformation-is-already-here/)[[192]](https://www.castlestech.com/)

This breadth follows from the hardware design. Android smart terminals now function as compact operating endpoints, not just card readers. That matters especially in Brazil because merchants often want instant settlement, lightweight business analytics, and mobility in one device [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[191]](https://www.castlestech.com/brazils-payment-transformation-is-already-here/).

### Why Brazil is different

The crucial mechanism is **fintech-acquirer subsidy economics**. Mordor says acquirers are shifting capital toward Android smart terminals that can converge EMV contactless and **Pix NFC payloads**, and are **subsidizing hardware in exchange for data-driven lending rights** [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market). In other words, the hardware is not priced to recover its full cost up front; it is priced to secure payment flow, data access, and adjacent financial-services revenue [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market).

Pix reinforces this. Mordor says **Pix by Proximity** launched in **February 2025**, extending tap-style interaction to instant payments [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market). Castles states the central bank launched **PIX Automático** and **PIX por Aproximação** in **June 2025**, expanding usage into recurring and tap-to-pay contexts [[191]](https://www.castlestech.com/brazils-payment-transformation-is-already-here/). The source counts differ—Mordor cites **42 billion Pix transactions in 2024**, while Castles cites **57 billion**—so the exact total needs current verification, but both support the same conclusion: Pix scale is large enough to shape device design and merchant economics [[44]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-america-pos-terminal-market)[[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[191]](https://www.castlestech.com/brazils-payment-transformation-is-already-here/).

### Typical price ranges

Brazil provides the clearest official-store evidence in the report:
- **Mercado Pago Point Smart 2**: about **12x R$16.65** or similar promotional variants [[198]](https://www.mercadopago.com.br/ferramentas-para-vender/maquininhas-point/point-smart)
- **PagBank Moderninha Smart 2**: **12x R$16.34** or **R$196.08 cash** [[201]](https://loja.pagbank.com.br/sem-celular/moderninha-smart-2)[[202]](https://pagbank.com.br/para-seu-negocio/maquininhas/moderninha-smart-2)
- **Ton T3**: **R$108 cash** or **12x R$9** [[204]](https://www.ton.com.br/maquininha/t3)

These are strikingly low compared with North American list prices because they are **merchant-acquisition prices**, not clean device ASPs [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[198]](https://www.mercadopago.com.br/ferramentas-para-vender/maquininhas-point/point-smart)[[201]](https://loja.pagbank.com.br/sem-celular/moderninha-smart-2)[[204]](https://www.ton.com.br/maquininha/t3).

### Installed-base proxies

Brazil offers unusually strong merchant-scale signals:
- **Stone**: **4.8 million active payment clients** in the 2025 IR snapshot; company highlights note **4.0 million clients** reached in 2024 [[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/)
- **PagBank**: **6.3 million active merchants** in Q4 2024 [[178]](https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/d1c190f7-52c1-4663-aa9f-0e0141aa5e3c/8c7bedf1-6e4b-cac1-e2e9-b143c156723b)
- **Pix**: **16 million businesses** using it after roughly 3.5 years according to Matera [[180]](https://20392958.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/20392958/AA%20US%20White%20Papers/Pix%20White%20Paper%20By%20Matera.pdf)

These figures are not additive because of merchant overlap and because many merchants accept payments through QR or tap-on-phone without a tablet-style device [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/)[[178]](https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/d1c190f7-52c1-4663-aa9f-0e0141aa5e3c/8c7bedf1-6e4b-cac1-e2e9-b143c156723b)[[180]](https://20392958.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/20392958/AA%20US%20White%20Papers/Pix%20White%20Paper%20By%20Matera.pdf).

### Estimated installed base

**Estimate (active tablet-style devices, South America):**
- **Low:** 2.8 million
- **Base:** 4.5 million
- **High:** 6.5 million
- **Confidence:** **Medium-low**

**Why this range is defensible.** Brazil dominates the regional base, and the **63.72% mobile/portable shipment share** indicates the center of gravity is already in portable smart hardware rather than fixed legacy estates [[44]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-america-pos-terminal-market). But the **4.8 million Stone active clients**, **6.3 million PagBank merchants**, and **16 million Pix-accepting businesses** cannot be read as hardware counts because overlap is substantial and many acceptance paths do not require a tablet-like device [[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/)[[178]](https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/d1c190f7-52c1-4663-aa9f-0e0141aa5e3c/8c7bedf1-6e4b-cac1-e2e9-b143c156723b)[[180]](https://20392958.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/20392958/AA%20US%20White%20Papers/Pix%20White%20Paper%20By%20Matera.pdf). The 4.5 million base estimate therefore assumes millions of subsidized smart/mobile POS devices in Brazil plus a smaller but meaningful base across Argentina, Chile, Colombia, Peru, and other markets [[44]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-america-pos-terminal-market)[[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market).

### Cross-regional synthesis

- **North America** is strongest where merchants intentionally buy a full commerce stack—hardware, software, and payments from one brand [[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[45]](https://squareup.com/us/en/about)[[46]](https://stocklight.com/stocks/us/nyse-tost/toast/annual-reports/nyse-tost-2025-10K-25670997.pdf)[[48]](https://s27.q4cdn.com/572064924/files/doc_financials/2025/q4/Investor-Overview-Deck-Q4-2025.pdf).
- **Japan and Korea** are stronger examples of **channel-shaped adoption**. Local rails, telco/acquirer packaging, and dealer-led installation influence which device class wins more than visible open-market MSRP does [[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market)[[196]](https://www.kisvan.co.kr/sub03_04_01.html)[[209]](https://www.paycas.jp/paycas-mobile).

### Pricing analysis

The key mistake to avoid is comparing these figures as if they were all **unsubsidized hardware ASPs**. In North America, many prices are fairly visible and reasonably close to retail list, especially for Square and Shopify [[14]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/shopify-pos-terminal)[[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare). In Brazil, the same merchant-facing class can be sold at **R$108–R$196** because the provider expects to earn back the subsidy through payment processing, merchant lending, and financial-services attachment [[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[198]](https://www.mercadopago.com.br/ferramentas-para-vender/maquininhas-point/point-smart)[[201]](https://loja.pagbank.com.br/sem-celular/moderninha-smart-2)[[204]](https://www.ton.com.br/maquininha/t3).

A second mistake is ignoring bundle structure. A **$149 Square Stand** is not a complete checkout device because it requires an iPad [[32]](https://squareup.com/us/en/hardware/compare). A **$349 Shopify POS Terminal** still assumes a paired tablet or phone for staff workflow [[14]](https://hardware.shopify.com/products/shopify-pos-terminal). Conversely, a **Clover Flex** or **PAX A920-class** device carries more of the payment stack inside the hardware itself [[28]](https://dharmamerchantservices.com/services/point-of-sale/clover-point-sale/)[[31]](https://docs.clover.com/dev/docs/clover-devices-tech-specs)[[85]](https://www.paxtechnology.com/a920pro). So “cheaper” and “more expensive” only make sense after separating **device-only**, **tablet-required**, **bundle-required**, and **subsidized merchant-acquisition** pricing.

## Installed Base Estimation Approach and Regional Estimates

### Regional sizing methodology note

Because exact installed-base figures are rarely disclosed, the report uses a transparent synthesis:

1. **Start with merchant/location proxies** such as Canada employer businesses, Korea merchant locations, Japan wallet-accepting terminals, or Brazil merchant-acquirer counts [[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market)[[167]](https://ised-isde.canada.ca/site/sme-research-statistics/en/key-small-business-statistics/key-small-business-statistics-2024)[[175]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/japan-payments-market)[[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/)[[178]](https://api.mziq.com/mzfilemanager/v2/d/d1c190f7-52c1-4663-aa9f-0e0141aa5e3c/8c7bedf1-6e4b-cac1-e2e9-b143c156723b).
2. **Filter to likely device-bearing verticals**: retail, restaurants, services, hospitality, healthcare admin, logistics, and field operations [[167]](https://ised-isde.canada.ca/site/sme-research-statistics/en/key-small-business-statistics/key-small-business-statistics-2024)[[170]](https://www.ihlservices.com/product/mobile-pos-mpos-market/)[[176]](https://go.2c2p.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/2025_2C2P-IDC-InfoBrief_AP242491IB_Final-1.pdf).
3. **Discount non-qualifying acceptance modes**: static QR, smartphone-only softPOS, basic non-tablet readers, or browser-based virtual terminal flows [[71]](https://en.komoju.com/blog/payment-method/cashless-shift/)[[73]](https://www.clover.com/pos-systems)[[91]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/brazil-pos-terminal-market)[[101]](https://www.nmi.com/developers/processors-devices/)[[177]](https://investors.stone.co/about-us/about/).
4. **Apply device-per-location logic**: restaurants and chains often deploy multiple endpoints, while micro-merchants may use one or none [[57]](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/south-korea-pos-terminal-market)[[170]](https://www.ihlservices.com/product/mobile-pos-mpos-market/).
5. **Cross-check against ecosystem-scale disclosures** such as Clover shipped devices, Square sellers, Toast locations, and PAX global footprint [[3]](https://www.paxglobal.com.hk/en/latest-news/pax-global-announces-2025-annual-results/)[[37]](https://www.clover.com/)[[45]](https://squareup.com/us/en/about)[[64]](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TOST/8-k-toast-inc-reports-material-event-2569dedba178.html).
